{{右| [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社正面.JPG|thumb||none|正面より]] [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社由緒.JPG|thumb||none|由緒書]] }}
'''止止呂支比賣命神社'''（とどろきひめのみことじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]にある神社。
== 御祭神 == *[[素戔嗚尊]] *[[稲田姫尊]]
== 由緒 == *創建年代は不明。しかし、[[延喜式]]にはその名の記載がある。 *承久3年 [[後鳥羽上皇|後鳥羽天皇]]が、当社境内松林内に[[行宮]]を立てて以降、'''若松宮'''あるいは'''若松神社'''と言われる。
==所在地== *大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4
==交通機関== *[[南海高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 史跡 == {{右| [[画像:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb||none|若松行宮跡]] }}
*若松行宮祉の碑 ==祭礼== *春祭（鎮火祭） 4月8日 *夏祭 7月11日12日 *秋祭 10月7日8日 *冬祭（鎮火祭） 12月8日
==その他==
[[Category:大阪府の神社|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の歴史|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]]

'''止止呂支比売命神社'''（とどろきひめのみことじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]にある神社。
== 御祭神 == *[[素戔嗚尊]] *[[稲田姫尊]]
===摂社=== *[[天水分豊浦命]]神社 *[[霰松原荒神]]社 :[[三宝荒神]]([[天水分命]]、[[奥津彦命]]、[[奥津姫命]])、[[大国主命]]、[[事代主命]]
== 由緒 == {{右| [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社由緒.JPG|thumb||none|由緒書]] }} *創建年代は不明。しかし、[[延喜式]]にはその名の記載がある。社名のトドロキとは当時の境内にあった橋の名前。 *[[承久]]3年([[1221年]]） [[後鳥羽上皇|後鳥羽天皇]]が、当社境内松林内に[[行宮]]を立てて以降、'''若松宮'''あるいは'''若松神社'''と言われる。それ以前は、[[住吉大社]]の摂社（奥の院）とされていた。
==所在地== *大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4
==交通機関== *[[南海高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 史跡 == {{右| [[画像:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb||none|若松行宮跡]] }}
*若松行宮祉の碑 *天水分豊浦命社社号標石
==祭礼== *春祭（鎮火祭） 4月8日 *夏祭 7月11日12日 *秋祭 10月7日8日 *冬祭（鎮火祭） 12月8日
== その他 == {{commons|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:大阪市の神社|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:住吉区|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=止止呂支比賣命神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|16.15|N|135|29|50.4|E|}} | 祭神= 素戔嗚尊、稲田姫尊| 社格=式内小社| 創建=不詳| 例祭=春祭（鎮火祭）4月8日、夏祭7月11日12日、秋祭10月7日8日、冬祭（鎮火祭）12月8日| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社正面.JPG|250px]]}}
'''止止呂支比売命神社'''（とどろきひめのみことじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]にある神社。
== 御祭神 == *[[素戔嗚尊]] *[[稲田姫尊]]
===摂社=== *[[天水分豊浦命]]神社 *[[霰松原荒神]]社 :[[三宝荒神]]([[天水分命]]、[[奥津彦命]]、[[奥津姫命]])、[[大国主命]]、[[事代主命]]
== 由緒 == {{右| [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社由緒.JPG|thumb||none|由緒書]] }} *創建年代は不明。しかし、[[延喜式]]にはその名の記載がある。社名のトドロキとは当時の境内にあった橋の名前。 *[[承久]]3年([[1221年]]） [[後鳥羽上皇|後鳥羽天皇]]が、当社境内松林内に[[行宮]]を立てて以降、'''若松宮'''あるいは'''若松神社'''と言われる。それ以前は、[[住吉大社]]の摂社（奥の院）とされていた。
==所在地== *大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4
==交通機関== *[[南海高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 史跡 == {{右| [[画像:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb||none|若松行宮跡]] }}
*若松行宮祉の碑 *天水分豊浦命社社号標石
==祭礼== *春祭（鎮火祭） 4月8日 *夏祭 7月11日12日 *秋祭 10月7日8日 *冬祭（鎮火祭） 12月8日
== その他 == {{commons|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:大阪市の神社|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:住吉区|ととろきひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:大阪市史跡連絡遊歩道歴史の散歩道]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=止止呂支比賣命神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|36|16.15|N|135|29|50.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神= 素戔嗚尊、稲田姫尊| 社格=式内小社| 創建=不詳| 例祭=春祭（鎮火祭）4月8日、夏祭7月11日12日、秋祭10月7日8日、冬祭（鎮火祭）12月8日| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[ファイル:止止呂支比賣命神社正面.JPG|250px]]}}
'''止止呂支比売命神社'''（とどろきひめのみことじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]にある神社。
== 御祭神 == *[[素戔嗚尊]] *[[稲田姫尊]]
===摂社=== *[[霰松原荒神]]社／[[天水分豊浦命]]神社（式内社） :[[三宝荒神]]（[[水分神|天水分命]]、[[奥津彦命]]、[[奥津姫命]]）、[[大国主命]]、[[事代主命]] [[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に合祀された。大阪市[[住之江区]]安立2丁目が元の鎮座地である。
== 由緒 == {{右| [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社由緒.JPG|thumb||none|由緒書]] }} *創建年代は不明。しかし、[[延喜式]]にはその名の記載がある。社名のトドロキとは当時の境内にあった橋の名前。 *[[承久]]3年([[1221年]]） [[後鳥羽上皇|後鳥羽天皇]]が、当社境内松林内に[[行宮]]を立てて以降、'''若松宮'''あるいは'''若松神社'''と言われる。それ以前は、[[住吉大社]]の摂社（奥の院）とされていた。
==所在地== *大阪市住吉区沢之町1-10-4
==交通機関== *[[南海高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]
== 史跡 == {{右| [[ファイル:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb||none|若松行宮跡]] }}
*若松行宮趾の碑 *天水分豊浦命社社号標石（大阪市指定文化財）
==祭礼== *春祭（鎮火祭） 4月8日 *夏祭 7月11日12日 *秋祭 10月7日8日 *冬祭（鎮火祭） 12月8日
==外部リンク== *[http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ 止止呂支比売命神社 公式サイト]

冬祭（鎮火祭）（[[12月8日]]） }} '''止止呂支比売命神社'''（とどろきひめのみことじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[素戔嗚尊]] *[[稲田姫尊]] == 歴史 == {{右| [[画像:止止呂支比賣命神社由緒.JPG|thumb||none|由緒書]] }} 創建年代は不明。社名のトドロキとは当時の境内にあった橋の名前から由来していると考えられる。また、稲田姫を『止止呂支比売』（トドロキヒメ）と呼んだことから由来する説や境内に流れていた小川がせせらぐ音、『轟く』（トドロキ）から転訛したことが由来とする説もある[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 津守廃寺跡・止々呂支比売命神社と古から繋がる地名 - 大阪府保険医協会]2019年3月19日　閲覧。[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。 元々は[[住吉大社]]の摂社（奥の院）であったとされる。[[1220年]]([[承久]]2年）に行われた[[後鳥羽天皇]]による熊野詣の際に、住吉大社神主である津守経国によって境内の松林内に[[行宮]]を立てられ「若松御所」と呼ばれたことから、'''若松宮'''あるいは'''若松神社'''と称すようになった[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html 止々呂支比売（とどろきひめ）命神社 - 大阪市]2019年3月19日　閲覧。 == 境内 == {{右| [[ファイル:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb||none|若松行宮跡]] }} *若松行宮趾の碑 *天水分豊浦命社社号標石（大阪市指定文化財） ==境内社== *天水分豊浦命神社（祭神：[[三宝荒神]]（[[水分神|天水分命]]、[[奥津彦命]]、[[奥津姫命]]）、[[大国主命]]、[[事代主命]]） :[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[無格社]]。霰松原荒神社とも呼ばれ、元々は大阪市[[住之江区]]安立2丁目に鎮座していたが[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）に合祀された。 ==祭礼== *春祭（鎮火祭）　4月8日 *夏祭 　　　　　 7月11日12日 *秋祭　　　　　　10月7日8日 *冬祭（鎮火祭）　12月8日 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[住吉区]]沢之町1-10-4 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[南海高野線]][[沢ノ町駅]]下車後、徒歩約1分 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} *[http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ 止止呂支比売命神社（公式サイト）] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきひめのみことしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:住吉区の歴史]] [[Category:住吉区の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市史跡連絡遊歩道歴史の散歩道]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The nam..."
revision.text:
'''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.<ref name="Osaka Health Association">[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref> Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}.
In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." <ref name="Osaka City">[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref>
== Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property).
=== Auxiliary Shrines === * **Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine**: Enshrines [[Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank.
== Festivals == * **Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)**: April 8 * **Summer Festival**: July 11-12 * **Autumn Festival**: October 7-8 * **Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)**: December 8
== Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
; Transportation * **Nearest Station**: Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]].
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine]
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank.

revision.comment: deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
revision.text:
'''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.<ref name="Osaka Health Association">[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref> Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}.
In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." <ref name="Osaka City">[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref>
== Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property).
=== Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank.
== Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8
== Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]].
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.<ref name="Osaka Health Association">[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref> Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}.
In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." <ref name="Osaka City">[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019</ref>
== Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property).
=== Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank.
== Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8
== Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]].
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine|Name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|Location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|Deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|Annual festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} [[Category:new pages]] * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; Location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

{{Shrine|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]]{{Munakata Faith}}[[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]

Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Totoro-hime Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July qq]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Aqqroximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July qq]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Aqqroximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
