{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=draft}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:c]] [[Category: c]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Totoro-hime Shrine}}

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=draft}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} with no former rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Totoro-hime Shrine}}

Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=draft|12=draft}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Totoro-hime Shrine}}

The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Totoro-hime Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shrine | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-zukuri|qq=}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|ja|10月7日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 7|qq=}}, {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 8|qq=}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 8|qq=}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 11|qq=}}, {{ill|July 12|ja|7月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 12|qq=}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|ja|12月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 8|qq=}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Toba|qq=}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano|qq=}} in {{ill|1220|ja|1220th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1220|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōkū era|ja|承久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū era|qq=}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|qq=}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|qq=}} ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku|ja|住之江区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminoe-ku|qq=}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanocho Station|ja|沢ノ町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawanocho Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōyō Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Kōyō Line|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historical Walking Trails in Osaka City]] }} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[en:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro Kibime Shrine]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shrine | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-zukuri|qq=}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|ja|10月7日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 7|qq=}}, {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 8|qq=}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|ja|4月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 8|qq=}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 11|qq=}}, {{ill|July 12|ja|7月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 12|qq=}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|ja|12月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 8|qq=}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Toba|qq=}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano|qq=}} in {{ill|1220|ja|1220th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1220|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōkū era|ja|承久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū era|qq=}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|qq=}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|qq=}} ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku|ja|住之江区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminoe-ku|qq=}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanocho Station|ja|沢ノ町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawanocho Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōyō Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Kōyō Line|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historical Walking Trails in Osaka City]] }} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[en:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro Kibime Shrine]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]

Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shrine | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]

Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 11|qq=}}, {{ill|July 12|ja|7月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 12|qq=}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|ja|12月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 8|qq=}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Toba|qq=}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano|qq=}} in {{ill|1220|ja|1220th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1220|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōkū era|ja|承久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū era|qq=}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|qq=}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|qq=}} ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku|ja|住之江区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminoe-ku|qq=}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanocho Station|ja|沢ノ町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawanocho Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōyō Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Kōyō Line|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historical Walking Trails in Osaka City]] }} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[en:Totoro Kibime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro Kibime Shrine]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Front view of the shrine hall|location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank=[[Shikinaisha]] (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ([[October 7]]–[[October 8]])|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|qq=}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines [[Kōjin|Sambaō Gongen]] ({{ill|Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, [[Suminoe-ku, Osaka|Suminoe-ku]], before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': A11roximately a 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Totoro-hime Shrine]] [[sn:otoro-hime Shrine]] [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shrine | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
