[[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}–{{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}})|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.60448611|N|135.49733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}, {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=July 11}}, {{ill|July 12|en|July 12|WD=Q2688|lt=July 12}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}, {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=July 11}}, {{ill|July 12|en|July 12|WD=Q2688|lt=July 12}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}–{{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}})|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.60448611|N|135.49733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]

Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}, {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=July 11}}, {{ill|July 12|en|July 12|WD=Q2688|lt=July 12}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7}}–{{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8}})|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.60448611|N|135.49733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja]][[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Todoroki Hime-no-Mikoto Shrine| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[Sumiyoshi Ward]], Sawanocho 1-10-4|Latitude degree=34|Latitude minute=36|Latitude second=16.15|N(South)=N|Longitude degree=135|Longitude minute=29|Longitude second=50.4|E(West)=E|Map country code=JP|Map=Japan Osaka|deity=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]]|Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor shrine)
Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7|1=October 7}}–{{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}})|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.60448611|N|135.49733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen|1=Kōjin}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732|1=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733|1=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7|1=October 7}}, {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=July 11|1=July 11}}, {{ill|July 12|en|July 12|WD=Q2688|lt=July 12|1=July 12}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8|1=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220|1=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era|1=Jōkyū}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|WD=Q13577821}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732|1=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733|1=Q135550733}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station|1=Sawanochō Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line|1=Koya Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Former [[Village Shrine]]|Establishment=Unknown|Main hall=[[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] style with cypress bark roofing|Alias=|Pilgrimage sites, etc.=|festival=Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7|1=October 7}}–{{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}})|Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[April 8]])
Summer Festival ([[July 11]]–[[July 12]])
Winter Festival (Fire Prevention Festival) ([[December 8]])|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.60448611|N|135.49733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|Sumiyoshi-ku]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) and was formerly designated as a [[village shrine]] under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to derive from the name of a bridge that once existed within the precincts. Another theory suggests that the name originates from calling [[Kushinadahime|Inadahime-no-Mikoto]] "Todorokihime." Some also attribute the name to the sound of a stream flowing through the precincts, with "todoroki" (thundering or roaring) evolving into its current form.[https://osaka-hk.org/posts/senjin125 Historical Overview of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka Health Association] Retrieved March 19, 2019 Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine, it is thought to have originally served as an auxiliary shrine (okunoin) to the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. In 1220 (Shōkyū 2), during Emperor Go-Toba's pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the chief priest of Sumiyoshi Taisha, Tsutsumori Tsunekuni, constructed a temporary palace (angū) in a pine grove on the shrine grounds. Known as the "Wakamatsu Gosho" (Young Pine Palace), the shrine subsequently came to be called "Wakamatsu Shrine." [http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen|1=Kōjin}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732|1=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733|1=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]
Main Shrine Building | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}{{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sawanocho 1-10-4 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_min = 36 | Latitude_sec = 16.15 | N/S = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_min = 29 | Longitude_sec = 50.4 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall={{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof | Alternate Names= | Pilgrimage Sites= | Annual Festival= Autumn Festival ({{ill|October 7|en|October 7|WD=Q2926|lt=October 7|1=October 7}}, {{ill|October 8|en|October 8|WD=Q2924|lt=October 8|1=October 8}}) | Rituals=Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}})
Summer Festival ({{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=July 11|1=July 11}}, {{ill|July 12|en|July 12|WD=Q2688|lt=July 12|1=July 12}})
Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8|1=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220|1=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era|1=Jōkyū}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Deities: {{ill|Sanbō Kōjīn|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbō Kōjīn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|WD=Q13577821}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732|1=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733|1=Q135550733}}), {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station|1=Sawanochō Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line|1=Koya Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:止止呂支比売命神社]] {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333}} [[Category:Buildings in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]
