[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009370.html Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine - Osaka City] Retrieved March 19, 2019 == Shrine Grounds == {{right|[[File:若松行宮祉.JPG|thumb|none|Wakamatsu Temporary Palace Site]]}} * Monument marking the site of the Wakamatsu Temporary Palace. * Stone marker for Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine (Osaka City-designated cultural property). === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tensuifun Toyoura no Mikoto Shrine''': Enshrines {{ill|Kōjin|en|Kōjin|WD=Q6455190|lt=Sambaō Gongen|1=Kōjin}} ({{ill|Mikumari|lt=Mikumari|lt=Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550732|1=Q135550732}}, {{ill|Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsu-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135550733|1=Q135550733}}), [[Ōkuninushi]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. This shrine, also known as Aramatsubara Arajinja, was originally located in Andatsu 2-chome, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, before being relocated to Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in 1907 (Meiji 40). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with no former Rank. == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival (Chinka-sai)''': April 8 * '''Summer Festival''': July 11-12 * '''Autumn Festival''': October 7-8 * '''Winter Festival (Chinka-sai)''': December 8 == Information == ; location * 1-10-4 Sawanomachi, Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ; Transportation * '''Nearest Station''': Approximatelya 1-minute walk from [[Sawanomachi Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Kōya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Site of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine | image=[[File:Todorokihimenomikoto shrine.jpg|250px]]

Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) ({{ill|December 8|en|December 8|WD=Q2301|lt=December 8|1=December 8}}) }} '''Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine''' (止止呂支比売命神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == The exact date of establishment is unknown. The name "Todoroki" is believed to originate from a bridge that once existed within the shrine grounds. Another theory suggests it comes from referring to Inadahime as "Todorokihime" or from the sound of a babbling brook that once flowed through the grounds, with "Todoroki" meaning "roaring" or "resounding." Originally, it served as an auxiliary shrine (inner sanctuary) of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. During {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}} in {{ill|1220|en|1220|WD=Q5392|lt=1220|1=1220}} ({{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Shōkū era|1=Jōkyū}} 2), a temporary palace called "Wakamatsu Palace" was erected in the shrine’s pine forest by {{ill|Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja|津守経国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori Tsunekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the shrine also being referred to as '''Wakamatsu Palace''' or '''Wakamatsu Shrine'''. == Precincts == * Monument of the Wakamatsu Palace Site * Stone Marker of {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|天水分豊浦命社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Designated Cultural Property of Osaka City) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Amemiketsutoyora-no-Mikoto 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly an {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Also known as Aramatsubara Kōjīn Shrine, it was originally located in {{ill|Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|ja|安立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andachi, Suminoe-ku, Osaka|1=Anryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451168}}, {{ill|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|en|Suminoe-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985711|lt=Suminoe-ku|1=Suminoe-ku, Osaka}}, but was merged into Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine in {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40). == Festivals == * Spring Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - April 8 * Summer Festival - July 11-12 * Autumn Festival - October 7-8 * Winter Festival (Fire Extinguishing Festival) - December 8 == location Information == ;Address * Sawanocho 1-10-4, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan ;Access * Nearest Station: Approximately a 1-minute walk from {{ill|Sawanochō Station|en|Sawanochō Station|WD=Q7428584|lt=Sawanocho Station|1=Sawanochō Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōyō Line|1=Koya Line}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} * [http://wakamatsujinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Todorokihimenomikoto Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|止止呂支比売命神社|version=82749980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11545333|co|commons:Category:Todorokihimenomikoto-jinja|ja|止止呂支比売命神社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
